mnemonics: PENDANT: A single leaf hanging from a branch. NULL: A graph with nothing at all.
PATHS are like a walk in the park (no repeats), CIRCUITS are a loop back to your car.
ISOMORPHIC graphs are twins in different outfits; same structure, different looks.
keyPoints: A pendant vertex is a vertex with a degree of 1.
A null graph (or edgeless graph) is a graph with no edges.
A path is a walk with no repeated vertices. A circuit is a closed path (starts and ends at the same vertex).
Two graphs are isomorphic if there is a bijection between their vertex sets that preserves adjacency.
commonMistakes: Confusing a null graph (no edges) with a graph containing a null vertex (which doesn't exist).
Assuming a circuit must be a simple cycle; a circuit can repeat vertices if it is not simple, but a path cannot.
Thinking two graphs are not isomorphic just because they are drawn differently; isomorphism is about connectivity, not drawing.
